Anyone who helped Mr Bamblett with the school garden last week will be given a
ticket to the Country Fair at the weekend.

Katherine: “That’s great. I helped Mr Bamblett with the garden last Thursday, so I’ll
definitely get a ticket.”

Kylie: “Fran told me that she is going to the Country Fair. She must have helped out
too.”

Funny pet videos are viewed hundreds of thousands of times a day around the world.
Many people clearly enjoy watching cats leap away from cucumbers in terror or dogs
falling off the backs of couches. But these videos are harmful, and should be made
illegal. Forcing pets to perform can be stressful and frightening for them. Furthermore,
the desire of their owners to achieve ever-greater numbers of views creates a motive to
film more and more extreme videos.

Which one of these statements, if true, most weakens the above argument?

options.text: Many pet videos feature funny things that were caught on camera by chance.
Trying to be popular on the internet can be bad for people’s mental health.
Many countries have strict rules governing the treatment of working animals.
The use of performing animals in circuses is generally thought to be morally wrong.

Chris and Li Li were both setting up dog-walking services in their local areas. They
both discovered that:

• The more people who know about our services, the more people are likely to
use them.
• If no one knows about them, then no one will use them.
• If we put leaflets in people’s houses, then more people will know about them.

out leafleting houses.”

Ryan: “And no one has been using Chris’s service, so he can’t have been posting any
leaflets at all.”

If the information in the box is true, whose reasoning is correct?

Daniella says: “Did you know that top tennis players can serve the ball at over 220 km
per hour? This means the other player has less than 0.25 seconds to see where the
ball is and then hit it. This would simply be impossible for a normal human being. I’m
telling you, some sportspeople must have superhuman reaction times!”

Which one of these statements, if true, most weakens Daniella’s argument?

options.text: Tennis players learn to anticipate where a serve is going to go before it is hit.
Only very few tennis players can serve the ball at speeds of over 220 km per hour.
There is quite a range of times within what is usually considered a ‘normal’ reaction
Reaction times in most sports are typically more than 0.25 seconds.

questions.stem:
Ellen is on board a ship, from which she can see a lighthouse. The lighthouse transmits
messages to ships by flashing three coloured lights in different orders. Ellen knows that
the possible messages are as follows.

Safe to dock: red, yellow, green
Storm incoming: blue, yellow, red
Do not dock: green, red, yellow
Another ship nearby: yellow, blue, green

The lighthouse flashes a message, but later Ellen only manages to remember two of
the flashed colours, but not the order in which she saw them. One of the colours is red.
This allows her to work out which message was conveyed.

What was the message?

The principal of a school says, “PE classes should include non-competitive sports such as
bushwalking and dance, and not just focus on competitive sports like football and tennis. Many

unhealthy rivalries.”

Non-competitive sports are just as effective as competitive sports at improving fitness.

questions.stem:
Children at John’s school were asked to complete a questionnaire about their sleeping
habits, to find out if they were getting enough sleep. The survey found that people who read
a lot at bedtime slept fewer hours (on average) per night than people who didn’t.

Which one of the following sentences shows the mistake that John has made?

options.text: It could be that people are reading more because they are having trouble sleeping.
It could be that people who don’t read as much spend more time playing computer games.
It could be that some children are not allowed to stay up late reading books.
It could be that some books are more exciting to read than others.

questions.stem:
Each card in a set of fifteen cards has one of three shapes on it: a triangle, a square or a circle.

Which one of the following statements must be true?

There are at least five cards with circles on them.

Ying thinks we should all buy soybean oil instead of palm oil for cooking. “I found out that
growing palm oil trees requires vast areas of rainforests to be cut down,” Ying says. “To help
save the rainforests and the animals that live in them, we must stop buying palm oil.”

Which one of these statements, if true, most weakens Ying’s argument?

options.text: Palm oil is also used in bath products such as shampoo.
People are using less oil in cooking than they used to.
Growing soybeans also involves the destruction of rainforests.
Palm oil is more frequently listed in recipes than soybean oil.

questions.stem:

If the information in the box is true, whose reasoning is correct?

Sara wants to persuade her cousin Rhiannon to come to the school play. Her only chance of
persuading her is if Sara’s brother, Sam, is playing in the school band. The problem is, it’s very
hard to get accepted into the band, since the standard is very high. Even some very talented
musicians have been turned away. You need to really impress the music teacher at audition to
stand a chance. Sam does not practice hard enough to get to that kind of level.

If the above information is correct, which one of the following is not possible?

options.text: Sam impresses the music teacher at audition but is not invited to join the band.
Sam practices hard but does not impress the music teacher at audition.
Sam does not impress the music teacher at audition but Rhiannon still comes to the show.

questions.stem:

some food for later.

I am hiding in one of three rooms. On the door of each room I have written two statements,
some true, some false. However, no more than one statement on each door is false.
Room 1
Room 2
Room 3
My name is Aziz
I am not in this room
My name is Luisa
I am not in this room
I am in Room 3
I am not in Room 1
Which one of the following statements must be true?

There are two ways to qualify for the annual Grant County Athletics Championship: by
winning at least three local events during the year, or by breaking a county record.
This year, 10 students from Lisa’s school have qualified for the Championship.
Lisa: “I know that a total of six county records were broken by our students this year. So that
means that more than half of our qualifiers must be record-breakers!”
Which one of the following sentences shows the mistake Lisa has made?

options.text: Some of the students from Lisa’s school may have broken more than one county record.
Some county records may have been broken more than once.
The number of county records broken this year may be higher than usual.

Some students who broke county records may also have won three local events or more.
